title: Economic Expertise and Rural Improvement in Iran, 1948-1963
source_org: Rockefeller Archive Center (research report by Gregory Brew, Georgetown University)
time_coverage: 1948-1963 (Pahlavi era, Point Four / US technical assistance)
units: narrative history with cited archival sources
license_terms: Rockefeller Archive Center research report, (c) 2017 Gregory Brew -- academic/archival use; cite author
notes: RAC Research Reports series, 8 pages. Covers the role of American technical/economic 'know-how' experts in Iran's oil-financed development program after the 1953 coup, centered on the Second Seven Year Plan (1955-1962), oil consortium revenue growth ($33m to $338m 1955-1960), and US Point Four-era rural improvement efforts. Secondary historical research report drawing on RAC's primary archival holdings, not itself a primary government document -- included per task instructions targeting this specific RAC publication.
